methods.name: Stage-Progression
methods.rationale: Applies typical Series B → next-stage step-up multiplier (1.9x) to $100M total raised proxy.

methods.name: Peer-Median
methods.skip_reason: insufficient narrow peer set in Transportation (found 0 same-stage, 2 adjacent-stage, 0 similar-tier; need ≥3)
methods.rationale: 5 same-sector peers exist in Transportation, but none of the narrow filters (same-stage / adjacent-stage / similar-valuation-tier) yielded ≥3 datapoints. Falling back to all-sector median would be misleading.

data.narrative:
Arc Boats is a Series B-stage transportation company founded by Mitch Lee and Ryan Cook that has raised over $100M to date. The transportation sector peer set comprises eight private companies, including Rivian, The Boring Company, Einride, Harbinger, and REGENT Craft, with no public comparables available for direct benchmarking.

The valuation analysis relies on a stage-progression methodology, which proved applicable given data constraints. Using Arc Boats' $100M in total raised as a proxy baseline, a 1.9x step-up multiplier typical of Series B-to-next-stage transitions yields an implied valuation of $190M. A peer-median approach was not feasible; while five same-sector peers exist in transportation, none satisfied narrow filtering criteria (same-stage, adjacent-stage, or similar-valuation-tier cohorts), requiring a minimum of three datapoints to be statistically defensible. Relying on an all-sector median would introduce material bias.

The triangulation analysis, constrained to a single applicable method, supports a valuation range of $162M (low) to $218M (high), with a median of $190M. The narrow method count represents the primary uncertainty; a wider peer set in the frontier transportation segment, or disclosure of Arc Boats' current revenue or unit economics, would strengthen conviction and potentially shift the range. Stage-progression multipliers also carry sector-specific variance risk that remains unquantified absent broader comparable data.
